ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a composite big-breakfast-with-pancakes set meal. FOODON_00002473 (breakfast food) is the closest defensible broad-category anchor. A more specific composite-meal term is not available in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for a pancake-based big breakfast set meal. A00KP (composite food) is used as the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor. Specific pancake or breakfast-set codes are not available in the public FoodEx2 catalogue as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite breakfast set meal. SNOMED CT does not catalogue mixed commercial meal sets at this level of specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate serving of approximately 550 g, comprising pancakes with maple syrup and butter, one sausage patty, scrambled eggs, and a hashbrown portion.

nutrient_source.title: Singapore Food Composition Database — Big Breakfast with Pancakes (F08020007)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, and cholesterol are taken directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry for Big Breakfast with Pancakes (cr_id F08020007) at confidence 0.95. This is an approved primary source under Project Veritas v2 source policy. Saturated fat (3.64 g/100g), trans fat (0.09 g/100g), and vitamin C (2.73 mg/100g) were originally enriched from a secondary non-approved source; for this record these three values are retained from the HPB input record as provided and flagged in notes as requiring verification against an approved primary source. No forbidden sources are used as primary evidence for any nutrient value in this record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite big-breakfast-with-pancakes set meal. GI is estimated at 66 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate component: pancakes made from refined wheat flour with maple syrup. The University of Sydney GI Database reports pancakes (from packet mix, refined flour) at GI approximately 66 (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283). The sausage patty, eggs, and hashbrown components contribute fat and protein that modestly blunt the overall glycaemic response, but the pancake-and-syrup component dominates the carbohydrate load. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (66) x carbohydrate per serving in grams (28.34 g/100g x 550 g serving = 155.87 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 102.9, rounded to 103.3 GL per 550 g plate. This represents a very high glycemic load per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 277 mg/100g with very low compensatory potassium (86 mg/100g, well below the 200 mg threshold) and low dietary fibre (1.67 g/100g); sugar at 5.36 g/100g with fibre below 2 g/100g further impairs sodium balance via insulin-mediated sodium retention.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal due to borderline-high sodium (277 mg/100g), critically low potassium (86 mg/100g), and low fibre — all three factors combine to worsen blood pressure control; if consumed, reduce portion size, omit maple syrup, and pair with high-potassium sides such as banana or leafy greens.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI refined-flour pancakes with maple syrup deliver 28.34 g carbohydrate/100g at GI approximately 66, with only 1.67 g dietary fibre/100g to blunt postprandial glucose rise; a 550 g serving delivers approximately 156 g total carbohydrate and a glycemic load of approximately 103.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This meal is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to a very high glycemic load per serving (GL approximately 103) driven by refined-flour pancakes and maple syrup with minimal fibre buffering; substituting wholegrain or oat-flour pancakes, eliminating maple syrup, and reducing portion size are essential modifications to make this meal safer for blood glucose management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 3.64 g/100g and trans fat 0.09 g/100g from butter and processed sausage patty; cholesterol 19.03 mg/100g; a 550 g serving delivers approximately 20 g saturated fat and 0.5 g trans fat, both atherogenic.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid or strictly limit this meal due to its combined saturated fat (3.64 g/100g) and trans fat (0.09 g/100g) content — a 550 g serving delivers approximately 20 g saturated fat and 0.5 g trans fat, which elevate LDL cholesterol and increase atherosclerotic risk; replacing the sausage patty with a lean protein, using olive oil instead of butter, and omitting maple syrup are the minimum modifications required.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sausage patty (processed red meat) is high in arachidonic acid (omega-6), amplifying TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways; butter contributes saturated fat; trans fat 0.09 g/100g is pro-inflammatory at any detectable level.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this meal in its current form — the processed meat patty is a hard RA dietary gate due to its arachidonic acid content, butter should be replaced with extra-virgin olive oil, trans fat must be eliminated, and cherry tomatoes (nightshade) should be monitored for individual inflammatory tolerance.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Big Breakfast with Pancakes is a Western-style composite breakfast set meal consisting of fluffy refined-flour pancakes served with maple syrup and butter, a grilled or pan-fried sausage patty, scrambled eggs, and a deep-fried or oven-baked hashbrown. The dish is commonly served as a single-plate breakfast or brunch offering in cafes and fast-casual restaurants across Singapore and internationally. The pancakes are soft and slightly sweet, the sausage patty is savoury and fatty, the scrambled eggs are creamy, and the hashbrown is crispy on the outside. Nutritionally, the meal is energy-dense at 261 kcal/100g, with a 550 g standard plate delivering approximately 1,436 kcal. Carbohydrate content is high at 28.34 g/100g (predominantly from refined flour and maple syrup), protein is moderate at 9.14 g/100g, and fat is significant at 12.33 g/100g including 3.64 g/100g saturated fat and 0.09 g/100g trans fat. Dietary fibre is low at 1.67 g/100g. Sodium is borderline-elevated at 277 mg/100g with very low potassium at 86 mg/100g. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 66 (medium GI) based on the refined-flour pancake and maple syrup component, but the glycemic load per 550 g serving is very high at approximately 103. This meal is clinically unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease without significant modification. It is not halal-certified in most commercial preparations due to the pork-containing sausage patty. Common modifications include substituting wholegrain pancakes, replacing maple syrup with fresh fruit, and swapping the sausage patty for a chicken or fish alternative.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry for this exact dish (cr_id F08020007) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C are carried forward from the HPB input record and flagged for verification. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on the dominant refined-flour pancake component. Overall record confidence is rated high given the primary HPB source coverage of all core macros.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (3.64 g/100g), trans fat (0.09 g/100g), and vitamin C (2.73 mg/100g) were originally enriched from a secondary non-approved delivery-platform source in the input record; these three values are retained as provided in the HPB input record and flagged for future verification against an approved primary source (HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central) when available. No forbidden source is cited as primary evidence in this output record. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, B-vitamin panel, fat-soluble vitamins, and moisture data for this composite breakfast set meal are not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry for F08020007 or in USDA FoodData Central for an equivalent composite meal as of 2026. 3. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate based on refined-flour pancakes with maple syrup from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283); no direct GI measurement for this exact composite meal was found in any approved GI source. 4. The sausage patty ingredient is assumed to be pork-based in the standard commercial preparation; halal status is therefore not confirmed. 5. Ontology codes are broad-category anchors only — no exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 code exists for this composite breakfast set meal; gap notes are provided for all ontology fields. 6. This dish is a Western-origin set meal served in Singapore cafes; it is not a Singaporean-origin dish and therefore does not require Singaporean multilingual naming (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) under G6 and Q1 of the scoring model.
